p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and to come John son of Michael of Methil sends greeting.

Let your whole community know me to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place now and to come, my whole land at Penshiel; for the same monks to hold and to have of me and my heirs in pure and perpetual alms for ever; as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, and fully held and possessed; in wood and plain, in lands cultivated and uncultivated, in meadows and pastures, in roads and paths, in moors, peat-bogs and marshes, in running waters and ponds, fish-ponds and mills, and all other easments that can be found or employed within the said land. And I and my heirs shall maintain and defend to the said monks the whole aforesaid land with all its liberties and just pertinents against all men; except for the land that I gave to the monks of May, and except for the land that I allowed to Henry Little and his heirs by its correct boundaries in the territory of Penshiels. The whole aforesaid land, as it is said, the aforementioned monks shall hold and have free and quit from every service, custom and exaction and demand of me and my heirs for ever.
